comments_critique.summary: The discussion revolves around the adaptation of the short story 'There Is No Antimemetics Division' into a film, with varying opinions on the source material and its execution. Some users express excitement about the film, while others critique the original story's depth and coherence.

comments.author: glenstein
comments.text:
I jumped into the book after hearing all the buzz as it seemed like the big scifi hit of 2025. Killer premise and for the most part the fast pacing works... for the first third or so. But it you can feel the short story anthology vibe as it goes on and becomes practically a disconnected series of vignettes, and even the central idea itself loses cohesion. The Big Bad Thing has so many manifestations that it feels like monster of the week rather than a true full book that holds onto a core sharply executed identity. Less a grand thesis than a series of isolated short form thrills.

So the long and short of it is, definitely worth it for the first third and dimishing returns after that.

As for the short film, I think it's great for what it is, gets better and better as it goes and is worth the short watch. Fascinating seeing the visual depictions of the stuff the book talks about both foreground and background.
comments.analysis: This comment provides a balanced critique of both the book and the film, highlighting strengths and weaknesses effectively.

comments.author: world2vec
comments.text: I just finished the book and it did not live up to all its hype. Quite boring, actually.
comments.analysis: While the comment reflects a personal opinion, it lacks detailed reasoning and may not represent the broader reception of the book.

comments.author: coldtea
comments.text: Did anybody else find the original story mediocre stuff? Interesting premise, but neither novel nor that deep. PKD has done 100x with much less.
comments.analysis: This comment raises a valid point about the depth of the story, but it is somewhat dismissive without substantial justification.

comments.author: the_af
comments.text:
Interesting that there are two versions that I know of!

I watched this one first, and I prefer it: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=w-IiVeGAydE. I like the actress who plays Wheeler better (no disrespect to Jasika Nicole, she does a good job too and I loved her in Fringe), and the script seems to stick closer to the original short story form from the website. Is this new short based on the book? If so, it seems the dialogues in the book are... weaker maybe?

Things I liked in this short: the use of B&W, and I thought the actor who plays O5-8 is stronger in this version.

I wonder if this story could become a "standard" for indie directors to riff off?
comments.analysis: The comment offers a comparative perspective on the adaptations, but lacks a strong argument for its preferences.

comments.author: manfromchina1
comments.text: Reminds me of "Total Rickall"(Rick and Morty: Season 2, Episode 4) which itself is probably a retelling of a retelling of a retelling of a retelling.
comments.analysis: This comment draws an interesting parallel but does not contribute significantly to the discussion about the film or book.
